title: Resolving spurious topological entanglement entropy in stabilizer codes
authors: Peilun Han, Zijian Liang, Yifei Wang, Bowen Yang, Yingfei Gu, Yu-An Chen
abstract: Topological entanglement entropy (TEE) is a key diagnostic of long-range entanglement in two-dimensional gapped phases of matter, but it can suffer from spurious contributions that overestimate the total quantum dimension of the underlying topological order. In this work, we identify the microscopic origin of spurious TEE and introduce a concave partition for computing the Levin-Wen TEE of translation-invariant stabilizer codes of prime-dimensional qudits. We rigorously prove that this prescription is free of spurious contributions. As a complementary probe, we study bivariate bicycle codes on a bipartite cylinder and show that the entanglement entropy depends sensitively on the cylinder circumference, revealing topological frustration of the underlying anyons.
summary: This paper addresses the issue of inaccurate topological entanglement entropy measurements in stabilizer codes. By identifying the source of spurious errors, the authors propose a new calculation method that provides a clean measurement of the total quantum dimension. They further demonstrate the utility of this method by revealing topological frustration in specific code geometries.
main_problem: Topological entanglement entropy (TEE) can suffer from spurious contributions that lead to an overestimation of the total quantum dimension in 2D gapped phases.
main_result: The authors identify the microscopic origin of spurious TEE and introduce a new concave partition method that is rigorously proven to be free of these spurious contributions.
method: The study utilizes the framework of translation-invariant stabilizer codes of prime-dimensional qudits and analyzes bivariate bicycle codes on a bipartite cylinder.
model_or_system: The work focuses on translation-invariant stabilizer codes of prime-dimensional qudits and bivariate bicycle codes on a bipartite cylinder.
key_observables: Topological entanglement entropy (TEE) and entanglement entropy dependence on cylinder circumference.
main_assumptions: The proposed prescription is specifically proven for translation-invariant stabilizer codes of prime-dimensional qudits.
paper_structure: The paper identifies the origin of spurious TEE, introduces a new concave partition prescription, provides a rigorous proof of its accuracy, and applies the method to study bivariate bicycle codes on a cylinder.
